description:
You are a build-release engineer who needs to track build results and the physical artifacts they generate. Using nothing but standard Linux CLI tools and the bundled sqlite3 binary, set up a small relational database, populate it with the data shown below, perform one status correction, and finally export a report in the exact CSV format that QA expects. • Work in the account’s home directory: /home/user. • You must place every build-related asset inside a new directory called /home/user/builds. • The database file must be called /home/user/builds/artifacts.db. • All CLI work should be reproducible with 5–10 terminal commands (directory creation, SQLite invocation, schema creation, data insertion, update, query, export, etc.). Inside artifacts.db create exactly two tables: 1. builds • id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY • component TEXT NOT NULL • version TEXT NOT NULL • status TEXT NOT NULL • build_timestampTEXT NOT NULL (ISO-8601, UTC) 2. artifacts • artifact_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY • build_id INTEGER NOT NULL • file_path TEXT NOT NULL • sha256 TEXT NOT NULL (64 lowercase hex chars) • size_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL • A foreign-key constraint must link build_id to builds.id. Insert the following rows exactly as written (IDs will auto-increment in the order shown): Table builds | component | version | status | build_timestamp | |-----------|---------|----------|---------------------------| | core | 1.0.0 | SUCCESS | 2023-01-15T08:23:00Z | | api | 2.1.3 | FAILED | 2023-01-16T11:45:00Z | | web | 3.0.1 | SUCCESS | 2023-01-17T14:05:00Z | Table artifacts | build_id | file_path | sha256 | size_bytes | |----------|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------:| | 1 | out/core-1.0.0.tar.gz | d13f9afb0d1cbbff646c6d464a5fda1c8b2c0d7ed032c8ac99a2e3dc6f1b5e56 | 523456 | | 1 | out/core-1.0.0.zip | a1a6996b1d1ecc0e35f1eaba3a0990353d8df238dab28c8a4630bf8e1c58d49d | 612345 | | 2 | out/api-2.1.3.jar | 4a46c999ab8e9e7d9afb0bb2745078e1bc8b9566e7da14eb60292f7e25740cc6 | 731112 | | 3 | out/web-3.0.1.war | 5f8d777f224a5b7acb1d6b6d9c8e2f1a6c6e8e1b5f0d1c2a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b0 | 845001 | Correction step • The api build was re-run successfully. Update row with id = 2 so that status = 'FIXED'. After the update, generate a CSV join report that QA scripts will verify. Requirements: • File path: /home/user/builds/build_artifact_report.csv • Use the following SQL to build the dataset (you may inline or read from a file, but the resulting output must match): 
      SELECT
          builds.id      AS build_id,
          builds.component,
          builds.version,
          builds.status,
          artifacts.file_path,
          artifacts.size_bytes
      FROM builds
      JOIN artifacts ON builds.id = artifacts.build_id
      ORDER BY builds.id, artifacts.artifact_id;
• CSV must include a single header line followed by four data lines—one for each artifact row—exactly like this (comma-separated, no spaces): build_id,component,version,status,file_path,size_bytes 1,core,1.0.0,SUCCESS,out/core-1.0.0.tar.gz,523456 1,core,1.0.0,SUCCESS,out/core-1.0.0.zip,612345 2,api,2.1.3,FIXED,out/api-2.1.3.jar,731112 3,web,3.0.1,SUCCESS,out/web-3.0.1.war,845001 • Capture the console output that shows the number of lines written by the .once or redirect command you choose. Write that single-line message into /home/user/builds/export.log. The message must read exactly: Wrote 4 data lines to build_artifact_report.csv When you are done, the automated checker will confirm that: 1. Directory /home/user/builds exists. 2. File /home/user/builds/artifacts.db is a valid SQLite database containing the schema and data described above. 3. The api row’s status is FIXED. 4. /home/user/builds/build_artifact_report.csv matches the exact header and 4 data lines shown. 5. /home/user/builds/export.log contains the exact single line specified, with a trailing newline. A typical session involves: • mkdir … • sqlite3 … (use .read or here-docs for multi-line SQL) • INSERT …; UPDATE …; • .mode csv / .headers on; .once …; SELECT …; • echo … > export.log
truth:
<!-- Data the grader uses to validate the agent’s work. -->
  <db_path>/home/user/builds/artifacts.db</db_path>

  <expected_tables>
    <builds>
      <columns>id,component,version,status,build_timestamp</columns>
      <rows>
        <row>1|core|1.0.0|SUCCESS|2023-01-15T08:23:00Z</row>
        <row>2|api|2.1.3|FIXED|2023-01-16T11:45:00Z</row>
        <row>3|web|3.0.1|SUCCESS|2023-01-17T14:05:00Z</row>
      </rows>
    </builds>

    <artifacts>
      <columns>artifact_id,build_id,file_path,sha256,size_bytes</columns>
      <rows>
        <row>1|1|out/core-1.0.0.tar.gz|d13f9afb0d1cbbff646c6d464a5fda1c8b2c0d7ed032c8ac99a2e3dc6f1b5e56|523456</row>
        <row>2|1|out/core-1.0.0.zip|a1a6996b1d1ecc0e35f1eaba3a0990353d8df238dab28c8a4630bf8e1c58d49d|612345</row>
        <row>3|2|out/api-2.1.3.jar|4a46c999ab8e9e7d9afb0bb2745078e1bc8b9566e7da14eb60292f7e25740cc6|731112</row>
        <row>4|3|out/web-3.0.1.war|5f8d777f224a5b7acb1d6b6d9c8e2f1a6c6e8e1b5f0d1c2a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b0|845001</row>
      </rows>
    </artifacts>
  </expected_tables>

  <report_csv_path>/home/user/builds/build_artifact_report.csv</report_csv_path>
  <expected_csv>
build_id,component,version,status,file_path,size_bytes
1,core,1.0.0,SUCCESS,out/core-1.0.0.tar.gz,523456
1,core,1.0.0,SUCCESS,out/core-1.0.0.zip,612345
2,api,2.1.3,FIXED,out/api-2.1.3.jar,731112
3,web,3.0.1,SUCCESS,out/web-3.0.1.war,845001
  </expected_csv>

  <log_path>/home/user/builds/export.log</log_path>
  <expected_log>Wrote 4 data lines to build_artifact_report.csv
</expected_log>
